description_long: This is a medium-sized kitchen knife featuring a straight, single-edged blade made of metallic material, likely stainless steel, with a sharp pointed tip. The blade transitions into a robust handle that is ergonomically contoured and appears to be constructed from a reddish-brown wood or composite material, secured with visible rivets. The handle is slightly curved for a comfortable grip and has a smooth, polished finish. The knife lacks any visible guard or crossguard, making it suitable for precise slicing and general kitchen tasks. There are no decorative engravings or embellishments on either the blade or handle, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, typical of a utility or chef's knife found in urban kitchens.
description: A medium-sized kitchen knife with a straight, pointed steel blade and a contoured reddish-brown handle, designed for practical use. The handle is smooth and riveted, with no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight blade and the contoured reddish-brown handle with visible rivets.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the narrow profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's edge and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handle's width and the alignment of the blade with the handle.
